a spell that allows you to turn your weapon into a magic weapon that deals an additional type of damage. The amount of bonus to attack rolls and the amount of extra damage increases with higher spell slots.
The target gains advantage on Constitution checks. It also gains temporary hit points equal to 2d6, which are lost when the spell ends.
When a creature targets you with an attack, saving throw, or ability check, you can spend your reaction to add 10 to your AC, to the Dexterity (Stealth) check, or to one ability check.

When a creature has advantage on an attack roll, ability check, or other task involving rolling a d20, the DM compares the roll to the DC or target number twice, using one of the rolls if the result equals or beats both comparisons.

1action
Time:
Range: 30 feet
Components: M(a of V,5, pinch powdered iron)
Duration: Concentration, up to minute1 action
Time:
Range: 60 feet
Components:
Duration:

If the target is a creature, everything it is wearing and carrying changes to match its new size. If the affected item or object reaches the maximum possible size in the space available, it remains that large. Until the spell ends, the target has double its normal Strength score for Strength checks and saving throws. The target's weapons grow to match its new size and deal extra damage. A weapon that deals ld4 damage becomes ld8. For weapons that have a bonus on attacks, the bonus increases by 5. Reduce works as Enlarge, except that it halves the creature’s size instead of doubling it.
This rule reduces damage to 0 if the calculated damage would bring a creature's current hit points below 1.
An action is a character's activity on their turn in the initiative order.
Not specified in the provided text.

The next time you hit a creature with a weapon attack before casting spell A, mass thorny vines appear at point of impact. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained by the vines until the end of the spell. Large creatures have advantage on this save. If the target fails the save, it is restrained and takes piercing damage equal to ld6 at the start of each of its turns. While restrained by the vines, the target can use an action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC. On success, the target is freed.
